message:
security(auth): harden SCRAM authentication against timing attacks

Improve SCRAM-SHA-256 authentication security with cryptographically
sound practices.

Security improvements:
- Use OsRng instead of thread_rng() for nonce generation
  (thread_rng uses ChaCha which is cryptographically secure, but OsRng
  draws from OS entropy pool which is preferred for security-critical uses)
- Increase nonce length from 24 to 32 characters (~190 bits entropy)
- Use constant-time comparison (subtle::ConstantTimeEq) for server
  signature verification to prevent timing attacks

The timing attack vector: An attacker observing response times could
previously recover the expected signature byte-by-byte by detecting
when early bytes match (faster rejection) vs later bytes (slower).

Also includes MySQL auth module improvements and Cargo.lock update.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -26,6 +26,7 @@ pbkdf2 = "0.12"
 base64 = "0.22"
 rand = "0.8"
 md5 = "0.7"
+subtle = "2.5"
 
 # Optional console support
 sqlmodel-console = { path = "../sqlmodel-console", optional = true }
